title: AN ACT CREATING AN ADDITIONAL BRANCH OF THE REGIONAL TRIAL COURT TO BE STATIONED AT THE CITY OF SAGAY, PROVINCE OF NEGROS OCCIDENTAL, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE SECTION 14, PARAGRAPH (G) OF BATAS PAMBANSA BLG. 129, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS âTHE JUDICIARY REORGANIZATION ACT OF 1980â³, AS AMENDED, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

chunks.text:
109 OG No. 2, 163 (January 14, 2013); Manila Bulletin; Philippine Star, November 19, 2012

[ REPUBLIC ACT NO. 10247, November 08, 2012 ]

AN ACT CREATING AN ADDITIONAL BRANCH OF THE REGIONAL TRIAL COURT TO BE STATIONED AT THE CITY OF SAGAY, PROVINCE OF NEGROS OCCIDENTAL, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE SECTION 14, PARAGRAPH (G) OF BATAS PAMBANSA BLG. 129, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS âTHE JUDICIARY REORGANIZATION ACT OF 1980â³, AS AMENDED, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the Philippines in Congress assembled:

SECTION 1. There is hereby created an additional Regional Trial

Court branch in the Sixth Judicial Region with seat at the City of

Sagay, Province of Negros Occidental.

The Supreme Court shall assign the branch number of the

newly-created branch of the Regional Trial Court in the Sixth Judicial

Region.

SEC. 2. The Chief Justice of the Supreme Court, in coordination

with the Secretary of the Department of Justice, shall immediately

include in the Courtâs program the operationalization of an additional

branch of the Regional Trial Court to be stationed at the City of Sagay,

Province of Negros Occidental, the funding of which shall be included

in the annual General Appropriations Act. The funds necessary for the

operation of the court herein created shall be appropriated and released

only upon the actual organization of the court and the appointment of

its personnel.

SEC. 3. Section 14(g) of Batas Pambansa Blg. 129, otherwise known

as âThe Judiciary Reorganization Act of 1980â³, as amended, and all other

laws, rules and regulations which are inconsistent with this Act are

hereby amended, repealed or modified accordingly.

SEC. 4. This Act shall take effect fifteen (15) days after its complete publication in the

Official Gazette

or in two (2) newspapers of general circulation.

Approved,

(Sgd.) JUAN PONCE ENRILE

President of the Senate

(Sgd.)

FELICIANO BELMONTE JR.

Speaker of the House

of Representatives

This Act which originated in the House of Representatives was

finally passed by the House of Representatives and the Senate on August

28, 2012 and July 24, 2012, respectively.

(Sgd.) EMMA LIRIO-REYES

Secretary of the Senate

(Sgd.) MARILYN B. BARUA-YAP

Secretary General

House of Representatives

Approved: NOV 08 2012

(Sgd.) BENIGNO S. AQUINO III

President of the Philippines
